jokes.text: Opening song lyrics: 'We're gonna fly to screw each morning'
jokes.setup: Community's typical self-aware opening song format
jokes.punchline: The nonsensical, overly optimistic lyrics that parody season renewal promises
jokes.explanation: Meta commentary on the show's survival and tone-shift promises, typical of Community's self-aware humor

jokes.text: Troy: 'If you want to get us a gift, we're registered at linens 'n things. We have plenty of linens. We mainly want the things.'
jokes.setup: Standard gift registry announcement for new roommates
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of the store name reveals they only want half the inventory
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with perfect economy - Troy's childlike literal thinking makes it character-specific

jokes.text: Dean Pelton: 'How you like me now, bitches?'
jokes.setup: The Dean has supposedly become tougher over the summer
jokes.punchline: His attempt at tough talk is hilariously out of character
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - the Dean trying to be tough is inherently funny due to his established personality

jokes.text: Dean: 'I'm gonna be a mean, lean, deaning machine.'
jokes.setup: Dean trying to sound tough and efficient
jokes.punchline: Substituting 'deaning' for 'killing' in the familiar phrase
jokes.explanation: Character-specific wordplay that fits the Dean's tendency to make everything about himself

jokes.text: Troy: 'It's probably the monkey that lives in the vents. I named him "Annie's boobs," after Annie's boobs.'
jokes.setup: Explaining a mysterious noise in the vents
jokes.punchline: Troy's innocent but inappropriate naming choice and his clarification
jokes.explanation: Troy's innocent crudeness creates perfect cringe - he doesn't understand why it's inappropriate

jokes.text: Reaction beat: Annie's horrified reaction to monkey being named after her anatomy
jokes.setup: Troy just announced he named a monkey after Annie's breasts
jokes.punchline: Annie's silent mortification
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man reaction that amplifies the previous joke's awkwardness

jokes.text: Dean: 'Homie don't dean this.'
jokes.setup: Dean trying to sound street-smart and tough
jokes.punchline: Substituting 'dean' for 'play' in 'Homie don't play this'
jokes.explanation: Another 'dean' pun that shows his clueless attempt at being cool

jokes.text: Jeff: 'Yeah, we have parted ways with our closest, oldest, craziest, most racist, oldest, elderly, crazy friend'
jokes.setup: Jeff describing Pierce's absence
jokes.punchline: The repetitive, escalating descriptors that become increasingly redundant
jokes.explanation: Jeff's verbose, contradictory description captures Pierce perfectly while showing Jeff's own wordiness

jokes.text: I'm back. How was your summer?
jokes.setup: Extended discussion about Pierce being gone forever
jokes.punchline: Pierce immediately appears, casual and oblivious
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with excellent economy. Classic sitcom entrance timing.

jokes.text: Pierce: 'I'm back.'
jokes.setup: Jeff just finished explaining Pierce is gone forever
jokes.punchline: Pierce immediately appears, undermining everything Jeff said
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and economy - two words completely undermine Jeff's verbose goodbye speech

jokes.text: Pierce: 'I underwent three months of intensive soul-rezoning at the laser lotus celebrity center.'
jokes.setup: Pierce explaining his spiritual transformation
jokes.punchline: 'Soul-rezoning' and 'laser lotus celebrity center' are absurd made-up concepts
jokes.explanation: Pierce's susceptibility to weird spiritual movements is perfectly captured in this absurd terminology

jokes.text: Jeff: 'You know what's magic about this table? It magically keeps our books from falling on the floor.'
jokes.setup: Pierce talking about the table having mystical properties
jokes.punchline: Jeff's brutally practical definition of the table's 'magic'
jokes.explanation: Jeff's cynical practicality perfectly counters Pierce's mystical nonsense

jokes.text: We'll see you when we see you.
jokes.setup: Jeff's speech about evolution and friendship
jokes.punchline: Anticlimactic dismissal disguised as evolved friendship
jokes.explanation: Beautifully constructed dismissal that sounds friendly but isn't. Classic Jeff manipulation.

jokes.text: Star-Burns: 'Yeah! The human being underneath it all, but no one's really interested in that, are they?' Jeff: 'No.'
jokes.setup: Star-Burns asking if anyone cares about his inner self
jokes.punchline: Jeff's immediate, honest 'No'
jokes.explanation: Jeff's brutal honesty cuts through Star-Burns' attempt at depth

jokes.text: Jeff: 'Excluding Pierce is what drove him crazy last year.' Britta: 'Wrong, being crazy drove him excluded.'
jokes.setup: Jeff's psychological analysis of Pierce's behavior
jokes.punchline: Britta's grammatically mangled but logically sound rebuttal
jokes.explanation: Britta's attempt to sound smart results in awkward phrasing but solid logic

jokes.text: Abed: 'Cougar town has been moved to mid-season. That's never a good sign.'
jokes.setup: Abed learning about his favorite show's fate
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary on TV scheduling that mirrors Community's own precarious position
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about Community's own scheduling issues disguised as character moment

jokes.text: Abed: 'Not cool, not cool, not cool, not cool, not cool...' Troy: 'Hey buddy, hey, it's coming back in January. Six seasons and a movie.'
jokes.setup: Abed's distress over Cougar Town's scheduling
jokes.punchline: 'Six seasons and a movie' - Community's own fan rallying cry applied to another show
jokes.explanation: Meta-genius - using Community's own fan slogan within the show creates layers of meaning

jokes.text: I was only allowed in the library one hour per day at the San Vicente correctional facility while serving a sentence of 25 to life.
jokes.setup: Professor explaining how long his degree took
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation of murder conviction
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered matter-of-factly. Good character establishment and surprise revelation.

jokes.text: Jeff's phone vibrating loudly during professor's emotional story
jokes.setup: Professor sharing moving story about grass growing in prison
jokes.punchline: Jeff's phone ruins the moment, then vibrates even louder when switched to vibrate
jokes.explanation: Classic cringe comedy - technology failing at the worst possible moment

jokes.text: Vibrates louder than the ringer.
jokes.setup: Jeff switching phone to vibrate to be polite
jokes.punchline: The solution makes the problem worse
jokes.explanation: Relatable tech observation that escalates the awkwardness. Good structure.

jokes.text: Jeff: 'Sean Penn called. He says to dial it back.'
jokes.setup: Professor Kane's overly dramatic teaching style
jokes.punchline: Comparing him to Sean Penn, known for intense dramatic performances
jokes.explanation: Perfect pop culture reference that captures the professor's over-acting

jokes.text: Dean's elaborate monkey hotel fantasy
jokes.setup: Dean complaining about monkeys on campus
jokes.punchline: Detailed fantasy about running a monkey hotel with banana buffets and tail holes in bathrobes
jokes.explanation: Dean's weird specificity about hypothetical monkey accommodations is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Annie: 'Wow, you've really thought this through.'
jokes.setup: Dean's elaborate monkey hotel plans
jokes.punchline: Annie's understated observation about his excessive preparation
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response that highlights the absurdity

jokes.text: Dean: 'Spray your solutions all over me.'
jokes.setup: Dean asking for brainstorming help
jokes.punchline: Unintentionally sexual phrasing typical of the Dean
jokes.explanation: Classic Dean innuendo - his obliviousness to sexual subtext is character-defining

jokes.text: Monkey knockout gas! Now that's the kind of grounded, sensible thinking I want to see this year.
jokes.setup: Dean asking for sensible solutions
jokes.punchline: Calling 'monkey knockout gas' grounded and sensible
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - the Dean's skewed perception of what constitutes sensible thinking.

jokes.text: Vice Dean: 'You bring your head down to my appendage tomorrow, and I'll show you "whazup."'
jokes.setup: Power struggle between Dean and Vice Dean
jokes.punchline: Unintentionally sexual invitation using anatomical metaphor
jokes.explanation: Accidental innuendo from the power-hungry Vice Dean

jokes.text: Britta's fake British show 'Cougarton Abbey'
jokes.setup: Britta trying to help Abed with a new British show
jokes.punchline: Completely made-up show with terrible fake British dialogue
jokes.explanation: Britta's well-meaning but misguided attempt to help, with hilariously bad fake British accents

jokes.text: Cougarton abbey. Just been 'round Cougarton chapel. I may be having an awakening. Come off it, Deidre. You've only been having a butchers at the choir boys.
jokes.setup: Britta trying to help Abed with fake British show
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top British dialogue that's clearly improvised and terrible
jokes.explanation: Perfect Britta - trying to help but making things worse with bad improvisation.

jokes.text: Leonard: 'All hail, sir eats alone.' Jeff: 'Shut up, Leonard. I heard about your prescription socks.'
jokes.setup: Leonard mocking Jeff for eating alone
jokes.punchline: Jeff's oddly specific comeback about Leonard's medical condition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leonard interaction - his mockery met with Jeff's weirdly specific knowledge

jokes.text: Movie titles that sound like poops: 'Remains of the Day', 'Green Mile', 'Operation Dumbo Drop'
jokes.setup: Study group goofing off instead of studying
jokes.punchline: Increasingly inappropriate movie title interpretations
jokes.explanation: Juvenile humor that shows the group's immaturity but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: Why did everyone on Cougarton Abbey just die? They only ran six episodes. That's the great thing about British tv. They give you closure.
jokes.setup: Abed confused about the show ending with death
jokes.punchline: Britta's twisted logic that death equals closure
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about British TV tropes with Britta's characteristic misguided confidence.

jokes.text: Jeff: 'You are human tennis elbow. You are a pizza burn on the roof of the world's mouth. You are the opposite of Batman.'
jokes.setup: Jeff insulting Pierce
jokes.punchline: Increasingly creative and absurd insults, ending with 'opposite of Batman'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating insults that become increasingly creative, with 'opposite of Batman' being perfectly absurd

jokes.text: Star-Burns: 'I live in the air vents of the school. Shhhh! I'm free, Jeff. Free!'
jokes.setup: Star-Burns offering to hang out with Jeff
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's homeless and living in the school vents, but considering it freedom
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about homelessness presented as liberation - very Community

jokes.text: Real Dean coming through... Oh, my goodness spacious,
jokes.setup: Dean entering the air conditioning annex office
jokes.punchline: His amazement at the luxurious space cutting off his tough-guy act
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dean's facade cracking immediately upon seeing luxury.

jokes.text: You could have lived the rest of your life in blissful ignorance and died a happy, pansexual imp.
jokes.setup: Laybourne explaining the Dean's former position
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and insulting description of the Dean
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly specific insult. 'Pansexual imp' is perfectly weird and character-specific.

jokes.text: Vice Dean: 'from nostril to rectum, now until the end of time.'
jokes.setup: Vice Dean intimidating the Dean with power metaphors
jokes.punchline: Weirdly specific anatomical route for his dominance
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortably specific threat that's both intimidating and ridiculous

jokes.text: Dean: 'I forgot everything you said before "rectum"!'
jokes.setup: Vice Dean's anatomical threat
jokes.punchline: Dean's admission that he stopped listening at the inappropriate word
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dean reaction - getting distracted by inappropriate words despite serious context

jokes.text: Vice Dean's team: 'That's a barber.' Vice Dean: 'Because I'm also sick of that ridiculous goatee. You look like a white Lou Gossett, Jr.'
jokes.setup: Dean thinks the barber is a lawyer
jokes.punchline: Specific racial comparison that's both insulting and oddly accurate
jokes.explanation: Oddly specific comparison that works because of its randomness

jokes.text: Jeff destroying the table with an axe
jokes.setup: Jeff's growing frustration with being excluded
jokes.punchline: Violently attacking the study room table with a fire axe
jokes.explanation: Shocking escalation that perfectly captures Jeff's desperation and the show's willingness to go extreme

jokes.text: Everyone knocked out by monkey gas
jokes.setup: Dean's plan to gas the monkey in the vents
jokes.punchline: Everyone in the school getting knocked out by the monkey knockout gas
jokes.explanation: Visual payoff to earlier setup, though more spectacle than clever writing

jokes.text: Shirley: 'I don't want to push it, but this would be a great time to baptize him.'
jokes.setup: Abed being unconscious and vulnerable
jokes.punchline: Shirley seeing it as a religious opportunity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Shirley - her ability to find religious angles in completely inappropriate situations

jokes.text: Britta: 'Why don't you go start a ruiners' club? Oh wait, you'd probably just ruin it.' Jeff: 'Well then, I'd be doing a good job because it's a ruiners' club.' Britta: 'You ruined my analogy.'
jokes.setup: Britta insulting Jeff with ruiners' club
jokes.punchline: Jeff's logical rebuttal and Britta's frustrated response
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of Jeff's lawyer logic turning Britta's insult against her

jokes.text: Blimey, inspector! Where have we wound up this time? The question isn't where, constable, but when. Inspector, look out! Blorgons! Eradicate! Eradicate!
jokes.setup: Abed discovering Inspector Spacetime (Doctor Who parody)
jokes.punchline: Perfect parody dialogue of British sci-fi tropes
jokes.explanation: Excellent Doctor Who parody establishing Inspector Spacetime. Shows Community's reference mastery.

jokes.text: Jeff mistaking Professor Kane for Pierce's rap artist friend Sugar Cube
jokes.setup: Jeff thinks Pierce orchestrated his expulsion
jokes.punchline: Revealing Jeff's racist assumption based on prison uniforms
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable but character-revealing moment about unconscious bias

jokes.text: Well, I guess they share one important feature in your eyes.
jokes.setup: Jeff confusing two Black men
jokes.punchline: Annie calling out Jeff's implicit racism
jokes.explanation: Good social commentary delivered through Annie's character. Calling out unconscious bias.

jokes.text: Jeff with the axe: 'I'm going to kill the one thing you love! I'm gonna kill it! As long as it breathes, it controls our lives!'
jokes.setup: Jeff's breakdown over being excluded from the group
jokes.punchline: Threatening to murder an inanimate table because 'it breathes'
jokes.explanation: Jeff's complete psychological break manifesting as threats against furniture

jokes.text: I'm going to kill the one thing you love! I'm gonna kill it! As long as it breathes, it controls our lives!
jokes.setup: Jeff's breakdown leading to attacking the table
jokes.punchline: Treating an inanimate object as a living enemy to be murdered
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Jeff's breakdown. Absurdist logic of 'killing' furniture.

jokes.text: Chang's speech about being excluded: 'it is a scary, lonely, Chang-filled world out there'
jokes.setup: Chang giving advice about exclusion
jokes.punchline: Describing the outside world as 'Chang-filled' instead of 'change-filled'
jokes.explanation: Classic Chang wordplay that makes the advice simultaneously profound and ridiculous

jokes.text: I can't believe Jeff attacked a table with a fire axe and still only managed to be the second craziest man in the room.
jokes.setup: Jeff's axe attack on the table and Pierce's revelation
jokes.punchline: Ranking the relative craziness puts Pierce above table-attacking Jeff
jokes.explanation: Brilliant perspective joke. Perfect structure highlighting the relative insanity. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: the sands of time. And the hungry seagulls of slowly growing apart.
jokes.setup: Chang's metaphor about leaving the tide pool
jokes.punchline: Overwrought extended metaphor with 'hungry seagulls of growing apart'
jokes.explanation: Chang's characteristic over-the-top metaphor. 'Hungry seagulls of growing apart' is brilliantly absurd.

jokes.text: Star-Burns: 'I'm a drug dealer, right? And you're a scientist, so couldn't we get, like, a Breaking Bad type of thing going?'
jokes.setup: Star-Burns trying to get back in biology class
jokes.punchline: Proposing to recreate Breaking Bad with the professor
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that fits Star-Burns' character while commenting on TV tropes

jokes.text: I'm a drug dealer, right? And you're a scientist, so couldn't we get, like, a Breaking Bad type of thing going?
jokes.setup: Star-Burns propositioning the professor for partnership
jokes.punchline: Direct Breaking Bad reference for criminal partnership
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture reference that fits Star-Burns' criminal tendencies. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Britta: 'I'm gonna study Psychology... I'm gonna be a therapist.' Group reaction of horror
jokes.setup: Britta announcing her career choice
jokes.punchline: Everyone's immediate horror at the thought of Britta as a therapist
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for future storylines while highlighting Britta's self-delusion

jokes.text: I'm gonna study Psychology. Like in a research capacity? No, in a hands-on capacity. I'm gonna be a therapist.
jokes.setup: Britta announcing her psychology major
jokes.punchline: Her plan to be a therapist despite consistently giving bad advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for future Britta therapy disasters. High character integration - she would choose the worst possible field.

jokes.text: Troy: 'Oh, interesting. So this is the year we all die.' Jeff: 'Yes, it is.'
jokes.setup: Britta's announcement about becoming a therapist
jokes.punchline: Troy's fatalistic assessment and Jeff's agreement
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending beat that captures the group's dread in deadpan fashion
